why_we_would: A P0 might choose this path to defer to P4's accusation and to avoid touching P2's Seer claim while it is still unverified, and the simulation shows the branch is forgiving: in 93% of worlds the wolf is still identified by Day 2 via renormalized beliefs, so the floor is not catastrophic. It also removes an aggressive early accuser, which a cautious villager could rationalize as reducing Day 1 chaos.
risks: With P(P1 is wolf) at only 0.04, this vote eliminates an almost-certainly-innocent villager 96% of the time, silences the one player who pointed at our top suspect, and gifts the surviving wolf a free night freeze; in the P4 world it loses outright.
